ai_summary.key_result: PC와 노동력이 모두 GPU 수요를 동반하는 agentic/physical AI 플랫폼으로 재정의되는 방향을 읽는다

relations.note: This essay reflects on the Jensen Huang SNU talk and links to the full transcript.

posts.title_en: RLDX-1: A Multi-Stream VLA Unifying Motion, Memory, and Physical Sensing
posts.title_ko: RLDX-1: 모션·기억·물리 감각을 통합한 정교 조작 VLA
posts.summary_ko: RLDX-1은 모션·기억·물리 감각을 멀티스트림 트랜스포머로 융합해, 접촉이 많은 휴머노이드 조작에서 π0.5·GR00T N1.6을 크게 앞선 RLWRLD의 정교 조작 VLA다.
posts.summary_en: RLDX-1 is RLWRLD's dexterous-manipulation VLA that fuses motion, memory, and physical sensing in a multi-stream transformer, far outperforming π0.5 and GR00T N1.6 on contact-rich humanoid tasks.

ai_summary.one_liner: A general-purpose dexterous-manipulation VLA that unifies motion awareness, long-term memory, and physical (tactile/torque) sensing in a Multi-Stream Action Transformer, with synthetic data and inference optimizations for real-time humanoid deployment.
ai_summary.problem: VLAs inherit broad scene understanding and language generalization from pre-trained VLMs but still fail on contact-rich real-world tasks that need motion awareness, long-term memory, and physical sensing — capabilities absent from standard vision-language pre-training.
ai_summary.solution: RLDX-1 adds a Motion Module and Memory Module to the VLM and a Physics Stream for torque/tactile inputs, fusing all modalities through a Multi-Stream Action Transformer (MSAT) with cross-modal joint self-attention, then trains it with synthetic data, a 3-stage pre/mid/post-training recipe (with optional RECAP-style RL), and CUDA-graph + kernel-fusion inference optimization.
ai_summary.key_result: On the ALLEX humanoid benchmark RLDX-1 reaches 86.8% average success vs ~40% for π0.5 (39.1%) and GR00T N1.6 (44.8%); on Franka Research 3 it reaches 68.5% average vs 34.4%/31.6%. Inference latency drops from 71.2 ms to 43.7 ms (1.63x speedup, ~22 Hz) on an RTX 5090.
limitations: Functional capabilities are injected through in-house teleoperation and synthetic data, so reproducing the gains requires private data and a large robot fleet.
Reported gains are on the authors' own ALLEX/FR3 benchmarks and tasks, making head-to-head comparison with other labs' setups difficult.
RECAP-style RL and test-time sampling help on some tasks but can degrade near-converged policies, so the recipe is not uniformly beneficial.

posts.title_en: The Mythos Export Ban, Sovereign AI, and the National Growth Fund
posts.title_ko: Mythos 수출금지와 소버린AI, 그리고 국민성장펀드의 명과 암
posts.summary_ko: 소버린AI는 필요하다. 그러나 소버린이라는 말이 특정 기업을 살리는 주문이 되어서는 안 된다.
posts.summary_en: Sovereign AI is necessary, but it must not become a spell for rescuing a few chosen companies.

ai_summary.one_liner: 소버린AI는 필요하지만 국가대표 기업 몰아주기가 검증을 대체하면 주권 역량은 약해진다
ai_summary.problem: AI와 로봇이 전략 자산이 되면서 한국도 독자 역량이 필요하지만 정책 자금이 몇몇 스타트업을 대마불사로 만들 위험이 있다
ai_summary.solution: 소버린 역량을 특정 기업 이름표가 아니라 통제해야 할 기술 레이어와 병목의 지도로 재정의한다
ai_summary.key_result: 국민성장펀드는 기업을 살리는 주문이 아니라 성능·고객·보안 기준을 통과하는 국가 작동 능력을 만드는 장치여야 한다

relations.note: Both essays discuss sovereign capacity and physical AI as strategic national infrastructure.

posts.title_en: ENPIRE: Agentic Robot Policy Self-Improvement in the Real World
posts.title_ko: ENPIRE: 물리 세계에서 스스로 개선하는 로봇 정책
posts.summary_ko: coding agent의 자동 연구 루프를 디지털 실험에서 실제 로봇 정책 개선으로 옮긴 시스템. ENPIRE는 reset, verification, rollout, evolution을 묶어 로봇 플릿 위에서 정책을 스스로 hill-climb하게 만든다.
posts.summary_en: A system that ports coding-agent autoresearch from digital experiments into real robot policy improvement. ENPIRE turns reset, verification, rollout, and evolution into a harness for hill-climbing robot policies on a fleet.

ai_summary.one_liner: ENPIRE turns real-world dexterous robot policy improvement into a coding-agent-managed physical autoresearch loop over reset, rollout, verification, and evolution.
ai_summary.problem: Real-world dexterous manipulation policy learning remains bottlenecked by human supervision for resets, success verification, experiment execution, and algorithmic tuning, while coding-agent autoresearch has mostly stayed in digital environments.
ai_summary.solution: A four-module harness: Environment construction for automatic reset/verification, Policy Improvement for heuristic/BC/RL/tool-calling updates, Rollout for single- or multi-robot evaluation, and Evolution for log/literature analysis plus code and training-infrastructure changes.
ai_summary.key_result: Frontier coding agents autonomously improve real robot policies up to 99% success; scaling from 1 to 8 agents cuts Push-T convergence from roughly 5h to 2h and pin insertion from over 1.5h to about 40min, at higher token cost.
limitations: Robot and compute resources are underutilized while agents read logs, write code, debug, or wait for model responses.
Fleet scaling reduces wall-clock time but increases token consumption super-linearly.
Reproduction requires integrated robot stations, reset/verification APIs, logging, and agent sandbox infrastructure.

posts.title_en: OmniRetarget: Interaction-Preserving Humanoid Retargeting
posts.title_ko: OmniRetarget: 상호작용을 보존하는 휴머노이드 리타겟팅
posts.summary_ko: 인간 motion을 휴머노이드에 옮길 때 몸 동작만 맞추는 것이 아니라, 손-물체-지형의 공간/접촉 관계까지 interaction mesh로 보존하는 리타겟팅 엔진.
posts.summary_en: A retargeting engine that preserves not just human body motion, but the spatial and contact relationships among hands, objects, feet, and terrain.

ai_summary.one_liner: OmniRetarget turns a single human whole-body demonstration into interaction-preserving humanoid reference trajectories by optimizing an interaction mesh with hard physical constraints.
ai_summary.problem: Humanoid whole-body loco-manipulation needs human-like references, but common retargeting pipelines lose object/terrain contact structure and produce penetration or foot-skating artifacts that make downstream RL brittle.
ai_summary.solution: Build an interaction mesh over body, object, and terrain points, minimize Laplacian deformation while enforcing collision, joint, velocity, and foot-contact constraints, then re-solve the optimization under object, terrain, and embodiment augmentations.
ai_summary.key_result: The method generates 8+ hours of retargeted trajectories and enables Unitree G1 policies to execute up to 30-second parkour/loco-manipulation skills with only five reward terms and simple domain randomization; Table II reports 82.20% robot-object and 94.73% robot-terrain downstream RL success.
limitations: The current frame-by-frame optimization is effective but future work may need joint trajectory optimization for noisier motion sources such as video.
Some extremely difficult motions still expose limits of the simple RL formulation, motivating curriculum learning or richer policy training.
The paper reports strong Unitree G1 results, but cross-lab reproducibility depends on access to comparable mocap, simulation, robot, and evaluation infrastructure.

posts.title_en: PHP: How Humanoids Chain Parkour Skills
posts.title_ko: PHP: 휴머노이드가 파쿠르 동작을 이어 붙이는 법
posts.summary_ko: 인간 파쿠르 motion을 motion matching으로 이어 붙이고, DAgger+RL distillation으로 하나의 depth 기반 휴머노이드 policy에 넣은 RSS 2026 논문.
posts.summary_en: An RSS 2026 system that turns retargeted human parkour clips into long-horizon humanoid references with motion matching, then distills them into one depth-based G1 policy.

ai_summary.one_liner: PHP composes retargeted human parkour skills with motion matching and distills privileged RL experts into one depth-based Unitree G1 policy for autonomous long-horizon humanoid parkour.
ai_summary.problem: Humanoid parkour needs dynamic whole-body skills, perception-driven timing, and long-horizon skill transitions, but reward shaping alone struggles to discover arm-supported climbing and isolated human motion clips do not teach when and how to transition.
ai_summary.solution: Retarget atomic human parkour skills with OmniRetarget, synthesize obstacle-adaptive long-horizon references via motion matching, train privileged single-skill motion-tracking teachers, then distill them into a single depth-conditioned student with DAgger plus PPO.
ai_summary.key_result: On Unitree G1 hardware, PHP climbs a 1.25 m wall in 3.63 s, performs cat/speed vaults near 3 m/s, adapts to about 0.5 m real-time obstacle displacement, and in simulation reports 0.95-1.00 success across 36/58/76 cm obstacles at 1.0 m/s and 2.0 m/s.
limitations: The pipeline currently lacks semantic scene understanding and richer conditioning such as language.
Real-world capability is constrained by a short-range, narrow-field-of-view depth camera at high running speed, causing perceptual ambiguity when obstacles appear too late.
The hardware lacks sufficiently strong hands or grippers for edge/bar interactions, so beyond-height climbing and hanging maneuvers are not tested.
